title: Fixing a Node.js Express Middleware Route for Dynamic Route Parameter Validation

statement.markdown: Write a middleware function for a Node.js Express server to validate dynamic route parameters. The middleware function should check if the route parameter is a positive integer. If the parameter is not a positive integer, it should throw a 404 error with a custom error message. If the parameter is valid, it should call the next function in the middleware chain.
statement.inputFormat: A URL path with a dynamic route parameter, e.g., '/users/:id'
statement.notes: The middleware function should be able to handle multiple routes with dynamic parameters. It should also be able to handle routes with no parameters.

hints.text: Use the `req.params` object to access the route parameter

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing a Node.js Express Middleware Route for Dynamic Route Parameter Validation
editorial.intuition: The middleware function should validate the route parameter before calling the next function in the middleware chain.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use a simple if-else statement to check if the route parameter is a positive integer.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a regular expression to check if the route parameter is a positive integer.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The regular expression should match positive integers only.
editorial.dryRun: Test the middleware function with different route parameters.
